metadata.notes: Content overhaul to Wingspan standard — Rogue B, March 2026. Sources: official Stonemaier rulebook, BGG FAQ, r/boardgames strategy threads.

key_rulings.text: When you Lead, you deploy a card to a location, gain that location's bonus, then MUST collect all cards from a DIFFERENT location.
When you Scout, draw the top card from any one location OR from the deck. You do not deploy a card or gain a location bonus.
The Sovereign token is claimed by deploying to Luna. Only one player holds it at a time. Worth 7 bonus points at game end.
Score points equal to your Fleet track position multiplied by 2.
Score 3 points for each Influence cube placed at The Institute.
The game ends when any player's Fleet marker reaches space 7 on the Fleet track.
The game ends when any player has placed their 7th Influence cube at The Institute.
The game ends when a total of 7 or more cards have been banished across all players combined.
When collecting, take ALL cards from the chosen location. That location is now empty until someone deploys there.
There is no limit to the number of cards you can hold in your hand.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Cards
- **112 character cards** (65×100mm) — Each card belongs to one of **14 Colors** from the Red Rising universe: Gold, Silver, Copper, Red, Blue, Yellow, Green, Orange, Gray, Brown, White, Pink, Obsidian, and Violet. Every card shows a character name, Color, a deploy bonus (triggered when played to a location), and an end-game scoring condition.

#### Board & Locations
- **1 game board** — Shows 4 locations in a row: **Jupiter**, **Mars**, **Luna**, and **The Institute**. Each location has a space where deployed cards accumulate face-up, and a bonus icon showing what you gain for deploying there.

#### Tracks & Tokens
- **1 Fleet track** — Printed on the board, numbered 0-7+. Advance your marker here by deploying to Jupiter.
- **6 Fleet markers** — One per player, placed on the Fleet track.
- **1 Sovereign token** — A large ornate token worth 7 end-game points. Claimed by deploying to Luna.
- **Influence cubes** — Small cubes in player colors. Placed on The Institute when deploying there. Worth 3 points each at game end.

#### Resources
- **Helium tokens** — The game's universal resource. Gained from Mars. Used for card abilities and worth 1 point each at game end.

#### Player Components
- **6 player reference cards** — Summarize turn actions and end-game triggers.
- **1 Rising token** — First-player marker.
- **6 House tiles** — Optional asymmetric starting powers. Each gives a unique ability.

#### Other
- **1 scorepad** — For tallying end-game scores.
- **1 rulebook + 1 solo rulebook**

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Setup
subtopics.content:
#### How the Game Works
Red Rising is a **hand-management combo game**. Your hand of cards at the end of the game IS your score — every card contributes its base point value plus any combo bonuses it earns. On each turn, you either **Lead** (deploy a card you don't want, gain a location bonus, then collect cards from a different location) or **Scout** (simply draw 1 card). The game ends when someone triggers one of three end-game thresholds.

#### Setting Up
1. **Place the game board** in the center of the table.
2. **Shuffle all 112 character cards** into a face-down deck next to the board.
3. **Deal 1 card face-up** to each of the 4 locations (Jupiter, Mars, Luna, The Institute).
4. **Deal 5 cards** to each player as their starting hand. Keep your hand secret.
5. **Give each player** 2 Helium tokens from the supply.
6. **Place each player's Fleet marker** on space 0 of the Fleet track.
7. **Place the Sovereign token** beside the board — unclaimed to start.
8. **Give each player** their Influence cubes (keep these in a personal supply).
9. **Optional — House tiles:** Each player draws 1 House tile for an asymmetric starting ability.
10. **Choose a starting player** and give them the Rising token.

#### Checkpoint
Each player should have: 5 cards in hand, 2 Helium, Fleet marker on 0, Influence cubes in personal supply, and a reference card. The board should show 1 face-up card at each of the 4 locations. The Sovereign token is unclaimed beside the board.

subtopics.title: Turn Actions: Lead & Scout
subtopics.content:
#### Your Turn
On your turn, you must do exactly one of two actions: **Lead** or **Scout**.

#### Action 1: Lead (Deploy → Bonus → Collect)
Leading has three steps, always in this order:

**Step 1 — Deploy a card.** Choose 1 card from your hand and place it **face-up** at one of the 4 locations. This card is now available for other players to collect. If the card has a **deploy bonus**, resolve it now (before gaining the location bonus).

**Step 2 — Gain the location bonus.** Based on where you deployed:
- **Jupiter** — Advance your Fleet marker **1 space** on the Fleet track.
- **Mars** — Draw **1 card** from the top of the deck into your hand. You may also take **1 Helium** from the supply.
- **Luna** — Take the **Sovereign token**. If another player has it, take it from them. If you already have it, you keep it.
- **The Institute** — Place **1 Influence cube** from your supply onto The Institute space on the board.

**Step 3 — Collect cards.** You MUST pick up **all cards** from a **different** location (not the one you just deployed to). Add those cards to your hand. That location is now empty.

**Example:** You deploy a low-value Brown card to Jupiter (advancing Fleet 1 space). Then you collect all 3 cards that have accumulated at Mars. Net result: you traded 1 card for 3 cards + 1 Fleet advancement.

#### Action 2: Scout
Draw **1 card** — either the top card from **any one location** or the top card of the **deck**. Add it to your hand. You do NOT deploy a card or gain a location bonus.

Scout is slower than Lead (1 card gained, no bonus) but doesn't require giving up a card. Use Scout when your hand is already strong or when no location has cards worth collecting.

#### No Hand Limit
There is no maximum hand size. Your hand can grow as large as you want.

subtopics.title: Locations, Colors & Resources
subtopics.content:
#### The 4 Locations
Cards accumulate at locations as players deploy to them. When you collect from a location, you take **everything** there.

- **Jupiter** — The Fleet advancement location. Cards pile up here between collections. Collecting from Jupiter can yield a big haul if several players have been deploying there.
- **Mars** — The resource location. Deploy here to draw a card AND optionally gain 1 Helium. Mars is the best location for building hand size and resources simultaneously.
- **Luna** — The Sovereign location. Deploy here to claim the Sovereign token. The Sovereign changes hands throughout the game as different players deploy to Luna.
- **The Institute** — The Influence location. Deploy here to place a permanent Influence cube. Cubes are never removed once placed.

#### The 14 Card Colors
Each of the 112 cards belongs to one of 14 Colors, each with a thematic identity:
- **Gold** — High base values (7-10). Powerful scoring conditions, often reward holding other Golds.
- **Silver** — Helium-focused. Grant or multiply Helium.
- **Copper** — Fleet-focused. Bonus points tied to Fleet track position.
- **Red** — Diversity rewards. Score for holding many different Colors.
- **Blue** — Influence synergy. Bonus points based on Influence cubes placed.
- **Yellow** — Flexible utility. Moderate values with versatile conditions.
- **Green** — Set collection. Reward holding multiple Greens.
- **Orange** — Interaction. May force opponents to discard or swap cards.
- **Gray** — Military. Often tied to Fleet bonuses.
- **Brown** — Workers. Moderate values, straightforward conditions.
- **White** — Sovereign synergy. Extra points if you hold the Sovereign token.
- **Pink** — Social. Reward having specific Color combinations.
- **Obsidian** — High-risk, high-reward. Large bonuses with strict requirements.
- **Violet** — Political. Tied to Influence cubes and Sovereign.

#### Helium
- Gained primarily by deploying to Mars. Some card deploy bonuses also grant Helium.
- Some abilities consume Helium for powerful effects.
- Each Helium remaining at game end = **1 point**.

#### The Sovereign Token
- Worth **7 bonus points** at game end to whoever holds it.
- Claimed by deploying to Luna — if another player has it, you take it from them.
- White and Violet cards often have scoring conditions referencing the Sovereign.

#### Fleet Track
- Advance 1 space per Jupiter deployment. Some card abilities also advance Fleet.
- Score: **(Fleet position × 2) points** at game end.
- Copper and Gray cards often grant extra points based on Fleet position.

#### Influence Cubes
- Placed permanently at The Institute. Never removed.
- Each cube = **3 points** at game end.
- Blue and Violet cards reference Influence cube count in their scoring conditions.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End-Game Triggers
The game ends when **any one** of these conditions is met at the end of a player's turn:
1. **Fleet 7** — Any player's Fleet marker reaches space 7.
2. **7 Influence cubes** — Any player has placed their 7th Influence cube.
3. **7 Banished cards** — A total of 7+ cards have been banished across all players combined.

When triggered, each other player gets **exactly 1 more turn**. Then scoring begins.

#### Scoring Formula
Add up all of the following:

1. **Card base values** — Sum the printed point value (1-10) on every card in your hand.
2. **Card combo bonuses** — Evaluate each card's conditional scoring text. Examples: "Worth 8 bonus points if you hold 3+ Red cards" or "Worth 5 points for each Gold in your hand." Add all earned bonuses.
3. **Fleet position × 2** — Your Fleet track position doubled. Fleet 5 = 10 points.
4. **Influence cubes × 3** — 3 points per cube placed at The Institute.
5. **Sovereign bonus** — 7 points if you hold the Sovereign token.
6. **Helium remaining** — 1 point per Helium token you still have.

**Total = Base values + Combo bonuses + (Fleet × 2) + (Cubes × 3) + Sovereign (7 if held) + Helium**

#### Tiebreakers
1. Most cards in hand.
2. Most Helium remaining.
3. Shared victory.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Core Principle: Your Hand IS Your Score
Every card in your hand at game end contributes base points + potential combo bonuses. Every deploy decision is a trade: you sacrifice a card's base value to gain a location bonus and collect potentially better cards. Deploy low-value cards. Keep high-value scorers.

#### Opening Priorities (Turns 1-4)
- **Examine your 5 starting cards.** Identify which Colors appear multiple times or have synergistic scoring conditions. This suggests your Color strategy.
- **Deploy your weakest card first.** A 2-point Brown card deployed to Jupiter gives you 1 Fleet advancement AND lets you collect cards from a juicy location.
- **Claim the Sovereign early** if no one else has. Deploying to Luna when the Sovereign is unclaimed is essentially 7 free points.
- **Collect from locations with multiple cards.** If Jupiter has 3 accumulated cards, deploying elsewhere and collecting from Jupiter is a 1-for-3 trade — massive value.

#### Color Strategy: Commit to 2-3 Colors
- **Gold + White** — High-value combo. Golds have the best base values, and White cards reward holding Golds and the Sovereign.
- **Copper + Gray + Jupiter focus** — Fleet-based strategy. Advance Fleet while holding cards that multiply Fleet scoring.
- **Blue + Violet + Institute focus** — Influence-based strategy. Place cubes while holding cards that score based on cube count.
- **Red + diverse Colors** — Rainbow strategy. Red cards reward holding many different Colors.
- **Green + Green** — Pure set collection. Greens reward having lots of other Greens.

#### Lead vs. Scout
- **Lead is almost always better.** You gain a location bonus AND collect cards. Scout only gives you 1 card with no bonus.
- **Scout when:** Your hand is already strong and you don't want to give up any card. Or when all locations have 0-1 cards and collecting would barely help.
- **Never Scout on turn 1.** You always have weak cards to deploy early.

#### Watching End-Game Triggers
- **Track everyone's Fleet position, cube count, and the banish pile.** The game can end suddenly.
- **Don't trigger the end before you're ready.** Reaching Fleet 7 or placing your 7th cube ends the game for everyone. Make sure your hand is loaded with combos before you pull the trigger.
- **If an opponent is close to triggering,** accelerate your own scoring. Deploy to Mars for extra cards, grab the Sovereign if you don't have it, and finalize your combos.

#### Common Mistakes
- **Deploying high-value cards.** A 9-point Gold deployed to Jupiter for 1 Fleet advancement is a terrible trade. Deploy 1-3 point cards instead.
- **Hoarding cards that don't combo.** A large hand of unrelated cards scores poorly. Dead cards = wasted base values with no combo bonus.
- **Ignoring Fleet and Influence.** Even if you're not building a Copper/Blue strategy, a few Jupiter or Institute deployments add reliable points that don't depend on card combos.
- **Feeding opponents.** When you deploy a card to a location, someone else will collect it. Avoid deploying cards that obviously fit an opponent's strategy.
- **Neglecting the Sovereign.** At 7 points, the Sovereign is worth more than most individual cards. If no one is contesting it, grab it and hold it.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Red Rising

**After Setup**: You have 5 cards in hand, 2 Helium, Fleet at 0, and unclaimed Sovereign beside the board. Each location has 1 face-up card.

**Turn 1 — Lead**: Look at your hand. Find your lowest-value card — maybe a 2-point Brown. Deploy it to Luna to claim the Sovereign token (7 points at game end!). Then collect the card at Mars, Jupiter, or The Institute — whichever location has a card that matches your Colors.

**Turn 2 — Lead again**: Deploy another weak card, this time to Mars. You draw 1 card from the deck AND gain 1 Helium. Then collect from a location with cards you want.

**Turns 3-5 — Build your combo**: Keep deploying weak cards and collecting strong ones. Look for patterns: if you're accumulating Golds and Whites, lean into that strategy. If you have Coppers, start deploying to Jupiter for Fleet advancement.

**Mid-Game — Read the board**: Check what's accumulating at each location. If Jupiter has 4 cards piled up, deploy elsewhere and collect the whole pile. Check opponents' Fleet and cube positions to estimate when the game might end.

**Late Game — Finalize**: Stop deploying valuable cards. Scout if your hand is strong. Make 1-2 final deploys to maximize Fleet, Influence, or Sovereign position.

**End-Game Trigger**: Someone hits Fleet 7, 7 cubes, or 7 banished cards. Everyone else gets 1 final turn.

**Scoring**: Use the scorepad. Add: card base values + combo bonuses + (Fleet × 2) + (Influence × 3) + Sovereign (7) + Helium. First-game target: 50-70 points is solid. 80+ is very strong.
